title: DeepCAD: A Deep Generative Network for Computer-Aided Design Models
abstract: Deep generative models of 3D shapes have received a great deal of research interest. Yet, almost all of them generate discrete shape representations, such as voxels, point clouds, and polygon meshes. We present the first 3D generative model that directly outputs shapes as sequences of CAD operations. Drawing parallels to natural language, we propose a Transformer-based network for CAD generation. We demonstrate capabilities in shape autoencoding and random generation, supported by a new dataset of 178,238 CAD models with construction sequences.
method_summary: Transformer-based autoregressive model that represents 3D shapes as sequences of CAD operations (sketch-and-extrude). Uses an encoder-decoder architecture to learn a latent space of CAD command sequences for both autoencoding and unconditional generation.

title: SkexGen: Autoregressive Generation of CAD Construction Sequences with Disentangled Codebooks
abstract: We present SkexGen, a novel autoregressive generative model for computer-aided design (CAD) construction sequences containing sketch-and-extrude modeling operations. Our model utilizes distinct Transformer architectures to encode topological, geometric, and extrusion variations of construction sequences into disentangled codebooks. Autoregressive Transformer decoders generate CAD sequences with specified properties, demonstrating capabilities in producing diverse, high-quality models while enhancing user control and enabling efficient design space exploration.
method_summary: Uses VQ-VAE with disentangled codebooks (topology, geometry, extrusion) to encode CAD construction sequences. Cascaded autoregressive Transformer decoders generate sequences with controllable properties from sampled codes.

title: Hierarchical Neural Coding for Controllable CAD Model Generation
abstract: We present a generative model that represents high-level design concepts of a CAD model as a three-level hierarchical tree of neural codes, from global part arrangement down to local curve geometry. A novel variant of a vector quantized VAE with masked skip connection extracts design variations as neural codebooks at three levels. Two-stage cascaded auto-regressive transformers learn to generate and complete CAD models from incomplete inputs.
method_summary: Three-level hierarchical VQ-VAE with masked skip connections encodes CAD designs into neural codebooks at part-arrangement, sketch, and curve levels. Cascaded autoregressive transformers enable both random generation and conditional completion.

title: CAD-SIGNet: CAD Language Inference from Point Clouds using Layer-wise Sketch Instance Guided Attention
abstract: We propose CAD-SIGNet, an end-to-end trainable and auto-regressive architecture to recover the design history of a CAD model represented as a sequence of sketch-and-extrusion from an input point cloud. Our model learns CAD visual-language representations by layer-wise cross-attention between point cloud and CAD language embedding. A new Sketch instance Guided Attention (SGA) module reconstructs fine-grained sketch details. The auto-regressive approach enables both full design history recovery and conditional auto-completion.
method_summary: End-to-end autoregressive architecture using layer-wise cross-attention between point cloud features and CAD language embeddings. Novel Sketch instance Guided Attention (SGA) module for fine-grained sketch reconstruction from 3D point clouds.

title: SketchGen: Generating Constrained CAD Sketches
abstract: Computer-aided design (CAD) is the most widely used modeling approach for technical design. The typical starting point is 2D sketches which can later be extruded and combined to obtain complex 3D assemblies. Such sketches are composed of parametric primitives augmented with geometric constraints. We introduce SketchGen, a transformer-based generative model that designs a sequential language for primitives and constraints, allowing distinguishing between different types and their parameters. The model can predict constraints for given primitives and generate complete sketches from scratch.
method_summary: Transformer-based autoregressive model with a custom sequential language for CAD sketch primitives and geometric constraints. Handles heterogeneous primitive types and constraint relationships in a unified sequence format.

key_results.improvement: significant over prior CAD sketch generation

title: Vitruvion: A Generative Model of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: Parametric CAD tools encode design intent through geometric primitives and parameterized constraints between elements. This relational specification functions as a constraint program, allowing edits to propagate coherently. We present a generative modeling approach for parametric CAD sketches trained on real-world designs from the SketchGraphs dataset. Our model autoregressively synthesizes sketches as sequences of primitives with initial coordinates and constraints referencing back to sampled primitives.
method_summary: Autoregressive transformer that generates parametric CAD sketches as sequences of primitives and constraints. Directly outputs constraint graph representations compatible with standard CAD software for downstream editing.

title: Mamba-CAD: State Space Model For 3D Computer-Aided Design Generative Modeling
abstract: We introduce Mamba-CAD, a self-supervised generative modeling approach for complex industrial CAD models that can model longer parametric CAD sequences. The approach uses an encoder-decoder framework based on a Mamba architecture paired with self-supervised pre-training on CAD reconstruction. A generative adversarial network then produces synthetic representations decoded into parametric sequences. We created a dataset of 77,078 CAD models and demonstrated effectiveness for generating valid longer sequences.
method_summary: Mamba-based encoder-decoder with self-supervised pre-training for CAD sequence reconstruction. Uses GAN for generation of latent representations that are decoded into parametric CAD command sequences, handling longer sequences than Transformer-based methods.

title: MamTiff-CAD: Multi-Scale Latent Diffusion with Mamba+ for Complex Parametric Sequence
abstract: We propose MamTiff-CAD, which leverages a Transformer-based diffusion model for multi-scale latent representations. The framework combines Mamba+ and Transformer in a custom autoencoder where the Mamba+ block incorporates a forget gate mechanism to effectively capture long-range dependencies. A non-autoregressive Transformer decoder reconstructs the latent representations. A diffusion model trained on latent embeddings learns distributions for extended command sequences up to 256 commands per model.
method_summary: Hybrid Mamba+/Transformer autoencoder for multi-scale latent CAD representations. Mamba+ with forget gate captures long-range dependencies; diffusion model generates in latent space for sequences of 60-256 commands.

title: GeoFusion-CAD: Structure-Aware Diffusion with Geometric State Space for Parametric 3D Design
abstract: We propose GeoFusion-CAD, an end-to-end diffusion framework for scalable and structure-aware generation. We encode CAD programs as hierarchical trees, jointly capturing geometry and topology. A C-Mamba block models long-range structural dependencies through selective state transitions. We introduce DeepCAD-240, extending sequence lengths from 40 to 240 commands while preserving sketch-extrusion semantics. The approach achieves superior performance on both short and long command ranges, maintaining high geometric fidelity and topological consistency where Transformer-based models degrade.
method_summary: End-to-end diffusion framework encoding CAD programs as hierarchical trees with C-Mamba blocks for long-range structural dependencies via selective state transitions. Introduces DeepCAD-240 benchmark extending sequences to 240 commands.

title: SketchDNN: Joint Continuous-Discrete Diffusion for CAD Sketch Generation
abstract: We present SketchDNN, a generative model for synthesizing CAD sketches that jointly models both continuous parameters and discrete class labels through a unified continuous-discrete diffusion process. The key innovation is Gaussian-Softmax diffusion, which applies Gaussian noise to logits and projects them onto the probability simplex via softmax, enabling blended class labels for discrete variables. Results show FID decreased from 16.04 to 7.80 and NLL from 84.8 to 81.33 on SketchGraphs.
method_summary: Unified continuous-discrete diffusion using Gaussian-Softmax noise process for CAD sketch generation. Handles heterogeneous primitive parameterizations and permutation invariance by jointly diffusing continuous coordinates and discrete type labels.

title: DreamCAD: Scaling Multi-modal CAD Generation using Differentiable Parametric Surfaces
abstract: We propose DreamCAD, which directly produces editable BReps from point-level supervision, without CAD-specific annotations. The framework represents boundary representations as parametric patches and employs differentiable tessellation for mesh generation, enabling training on large-scale 3D datasets. We introduce CADCap-1M, the largest CAD captioning dataset with 1M+ descriptions generated using GPT-5. The method achieves state-of-the-art results on ABC and Objaverse benchmarks across text, image, and point modalities.
method_summary: Represents BReps as differentiable parametric patches (Bezier surfaces) with differentiable tessellation enabling training on mesh datasets without CAD annotations. Multi-modal conditioning via text, image, and point cloud inputs. Introduces CADCap-1M captioning dataset.

title: GenCAD: Image-Conditioned Computer-Aided Design Generation with Transformer-Based Contrastive Representation and Diffusion Priors
abstract: We introduce GenCAD, a generative model that employs autoregressive transformers with a contrastive learning framework and latent diffusion models to transform image inputs into parametric CAD command sequences. The approach generates editable 3D shape representations, outperforming existing methods in both unconditional and conditional CAD generation, and enables retrieval of CAD models using image queries from large CAD databases.
method_summary: Combines autoregressive transformer with contrastive learning and latent diffusion priors for image-to-CAD generation. Uses CLIP-like contrastive training to align image and CAD sequence representations, then diffusion model generates in latent space.

title: RECAD: Revisiting CAD Model Generation by Learning Raster Sketch
abstract: We introduce RECAD, a framework that generates CAD models by producing raster sketches and 3D extrusions. Rather than using discrete sequences of parametric segments, the approach represents sketches as raster images, offering enhanced geometric representation without constraints on line/curve types, interpolation within continuous latent space, and more intuitive user control. Two diffusion networks generate extrusion boxes and sketch images respectively.
method_summary: Dual diffusion network framework: first network generates extrusion boxes conditioned on parameters, second produces raster sketch images conditioned on boxes. Represents CAD sketches as raster images instead of parametric sequences for more flexible geometry.

title: FlexCAD: Unified and Versatile Controllable CAD Generation with Fine-tuned Large Language Models
abstract: We propose FlexCAD, which fine-tunes large language models for controllable CAD generation. The approach represents CAD models as structured text, abstracting each hierarchy as token sequences. Hierarchy-aware masking during training masks specific CAD text fields and trains the LLM to predict them. At inference, user intent converts to CAD text with masked sections. Supports control across sketch-extrusion, extrusion, sketch, face, loop and curve levels.
method_summary: Fine-tunes LLMs on CAD models represented as structured text. Hierarchy-aware masking strategy enables controllable generation at multiple CAD construction levels (sketch-extrusion, extrusion, sketch, face, loop, curve) within a single unified model.

title: Feasibility Enhancement for 3D CAD Generation
abstract: This framework integrates a guided diffusion denoising process in the latent space and a regression-based correction mechanism to refine infeasible CAD command sequences while preserving geometric accuracy. By significantly improving the feasibility rate of generating CAD models, our approach helps expand the availability of high-quality training data and enhances the applicability of AI-driven CAD generation.
method_summary: Post-processing framework combining guided diffusion denoising in latent space with regression-based correction to fix infeasible CAD command sequences. Improves validity rate of generated CAD models without sacrificing geometric accuracy.

title: BrepGen: A B-rep Generative Diffusion Model with Structured Latent Geometry
abstract: We present BrepGen, a diffusion-based generative approach that directly outputs Boundary representation (B-rep) CAD models. BrepGen represents a B-rep model as a novel structured latent geometry in a hierarchical tree. With the root node representing a whole CAD solid, each element (face, edge, vertex) stores global bounding box information and latent codes for local geometry. Topology is implicitly captured through node duplication. Transformer-based diffusion sequentially denoises node features while detecting and merging duplicates to recover B-rep topology.
method_summary: Hierarchical tree representation of B-rep with latent geometry codes per face/edge/vertex. Transformer-based diffusion denoises node features; topology recovered by detecting and merging duplicate nodes. First to incorporate free-form and doubly-curved surfaces in CAD generation.

key_results.novelty: first free-form surfaces in CAD generation

title: HiDiGen: Hierarchical Diffusion for B-Rep Generation with Explicit Topological Constraints
abstract: We propose HiDiGen, a two-stage hierarchical framework that decouples geometry modeling while maintaining explicit topological constraints. The first stage establishes face-edge incidence relations and generates face proxies and initial edge curves. The second stage employs Transformer-based diffusion modules to refine geometry by generating precise face surfaces and vertex positions, with edge-vertex adjacencies dynamically enforced.
method_summary: Two-stage hierarchical diffusion: Stage 1 generates topology (face-edge incidence) and coarse geometry; Stage 2 refines with Transformer-based diffusion for precise surfaces and vertices with dynamically enforced topological constraints.

title: DTGBrepGen: A Novel B-rep Generative Model through Decoupling Topology and Geometry
abstract: We propose DTGBrepGen, a novel topology-geometry decoupled framework for B-rep generation. Our approach first generates valid topological structures through a two-stage process modeling adjacency relationships, then uses Transformer-based diffusion models to sequentially generate vertex coordinates, edge geometries, and face geometries as B-splines.
method_summary: Decoupled topology-geometry framework: first generates valid topological structures via two sequential Transformer encoder-decoders for adjacency relations, then Transformer-based diffusion generates vertex/edge/face geometries as B-splines.

title: AutoBrep: Autoregressive B-Rep Generation with Unified Topology and Geometry
abstract: We present AutoBrep, a novel Transformer model that autoregressively generates B-Reps with high quality and validity. AutoBrep employs a unified tokenization scheme that encodes both geometric and topological characteristics of a B-Rep model as a sequence of discrete tokens. Geometric primitives are encoded as latent geometry tokens, and structural relationships as special topological reference tokens. Sequence order follows breadth-first traversal of the B-Rep face adjacency graph.
method_summary: Decoder-only Transformer with unified tokenization encoding both geometry (latent tokens) and topology (reference tokens) of B-rep models. Breadth-first traversal ordering enables progressive face-by-face generation with native autocomplete support.

title: HoLa: B-Rep Generation using a Holistic Latent Representation
abstract: We introduce a novel representation for learning and generating CAD models as boundary representations (B-Reps). The key innovation is a holistic latent (HoLa) space that unifies continuous geometric properties of B-Rep primitives and their discrete topological relations. We observe that the topological connection between two surfaces is intrinsically tied to the geometry of their intersecting curve, enabling reformulation of topology learning as geometric reconstruction. The compact representation supports a diffusion-based generator accepting point clouds, images, sketches, and text prompts, achieving 82% validity vs ~50% for prior work.
method_summary: Holistic latent space unifying B-rep geometry and topology by deriving curve geometries from surface pairs via neural intersection network. Eliminates explicit curves/vertices/topology from latent space. Diffusion-based generator supports multi-modal conditioning.

title: BrepGPT: Autoregressive B-rep Generation with Voronoi Half-Patch
abstract: We present BrepGPT, a single-stage autoregressive framework for B-rep generation. Our key innovation is the Voronoi Half-Patch (VHP) representation, which decomposes B-reps into unified local units by assigning geometry to nearest half-edges and sampling their next pointers. Dual VQ-VAEs encode vertex topology and Voronoi Half-Patches into vertex-based tokens. A decoder-only Transformer autoregressively predicts these tokens for complete B-rep generation.
method_summary: Single-stage autoregressive B-rep generation using Voronoi Half-Patch (VHP) representation that unifies geometry and topology in local units. Dual VQ-VAEs encode vertex topology and patches; decoder-only Transformer generates token sequences.

title: BrepARG: AutoRegressive Generation with B-rep Holistic Token Sequence Representation
abstract: We propose BrepARG, the first attempt to encode B-rep geometry and topology into a holistic token sequence representation, enabling sequence-based B-rep generation with an autoregressive architecture. Three token types (geometry, position, face index) are organized hierarchically. A transformer-based decoder-only model with causal masking learns token distributions through next-token prediction.
method_summary: Encodes complete B-rep geometry and topology into holistic token sequences with three token types (geometry, position, face index). Decoder-only Transformer with causal masking generates B-reps via next-token prediction.

title: Flatten The Complex: Joint B-Rep Generation via Compositional k-Cell Particles
abstract: We introduce a novel paradigm that reformulates B-Reps into sets of compositional k-cell particles. Our approach encodes each topological entity as a composition of particles, where adjacent cells share identical latents at their interfaces, promoting geometric coupling along shared boundaries. By decoupling the rigid hierarchy, our representation unifies vertices, edges, and faces, enabling joint generation of topology and geometry with global context awareness. Multi-modal flow matching framework handles unconditional and conditional tasks.
method_summary: Reformulates B-reps as compositional k-cell particles where adjacent cells share latents at interfaces. Multi-modal flow matching generates particle sets jointly, enabling topology-geometry co-generation with global context. Supports non-manifold structures.

title: CSGNet: Neural Shape Parser for Constructive Solid Geometry
abstract: We present a neural architecture that takes as input a 2D or 3D shape and outputs a program that generates the shape using constructive solid geometry principles. Rather than relying on bottom-up primitive detection, the model employs a recurrent neural network for top-down shape parsing, delivering faster results and more interpretable modeling instructions. The approach can be trained on novel datasets lacking ground-truth program annotations through policy gradient techniques.
method_summary: RNN-based encoder-decoder that parses 2D/3D shapes into CSG programs (boolean operations on primitives). Top-down approach using recurrent generation of CSG instructions. Trainable without program annotations via policy gradient.

title: CSGNe: Neural Shape Parsers for Constructive Solid Geometry
abstract: Constructive Solid Geometry (CSG) is a geometric modeling technique that defines complex shapes by recursively applying boolean operations on primitives. We present CSGNe, a deep network that takes as input a 2D or 3D shape and outputs a CSG program. We explore two architectures: a standard CNN-RNN encoder-decoder and an enhanced version with a memory module based on program execution stacks.
method_summary: Extended CSGNet with memory module based on program execution stacks for improved CSG program generation. CNN-RNN architecture with stack-based memory enables more complex shape decompositions.

title: UCSG-Net: Unsupervised Discovering of Constructive Solid Geometry Tree
abstract: We propose UCSG-Net, a model that extracts a CSG parse tree without any supervision. The approach predicts parameters of primitives, binarizes their signed distance representations through a differentiable indicator function, and simultaneously discovers the Boolean operator tree structure. The model dynamically selects operator combinations that best reconstruct the input.
method_summary: Unsupervised CSG tree discovery using differentiable indicator functions on primitive SDFs. Dynamically selects boolean operator combinations without ground-truth CSG annotations. Produces interpretable parse trees compatible with CAD software.

title: CSG-Stump: A Learning Friendly CSG-Like Representation for Interpretable Shape Parsing
abstract: We present CSG-Stump Net, an unsupervised end-to-end network for learning 3D shapes from point clouds. CSG-Stump is a three-level structure combining complement, intersection, and union layers. It is proven equivalent to CSG in representation, inheriting interpretable, compact and editable nature while freeing from complex tree structures.
method_summary: Three-level CSG-like representation (complement-intersection-union) that is provably equivalent to CSG trees but with constant-dimensionality output. Unsupervised learning from point clouds without CSG annotations.

title: D2CSG: Unsupervised Learning of Compact CSG Trees with Dual Complements and Dropouts
abstract: We present D2CSG, a neural model with two dual and complementary network branches with dropouts for unsupervised learning of compact CSG representations. The network reconstructs shapes through fixed-order assembly of quadric primitives, with a dedicated residual branch for shape complement subtraction. Weight dropout enhances CSG tree compactness by eliminating redundant primitives.
method_summary: Dual-branch neural network for unsupervised CSG learning: cover branch models overall shape, residual branch handles complex complement via subtraction. Dropout mechanism eliminates redundant primitives for compact CSG trees. Provably general representation.

title: CADDreamer: CAD Object Generation from Single-view Images
abstract: We introduce CADDreamer, a novel approach for generating boundary representations (B-rep) of CAD objects from a single image. CADDreamer employs a primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model that captures both local geometric details and high-level structural semantics. By encoding primitive information into color channels, the method leverages pre-trained diffusion priors to infer multi-view normal and semantic maps. Geometric optimization and topology-preserving extraction produce compact CAD objects with sharp edges and watertight topology.
method_summary: Primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model for single-image to B-rep generation. Encodes primitive semantics in color channels to leverage pre-trained diffusion priors. Topology-preserving extraction produces watertight B-rep CAD objects.

title: NURBGen: High-Fidelity Text-to-CAD Generation through LLM-Driven NURBS Modeling
abstract: We present NURBGen, the first framework for generating editable 3D CAD models directly from natural language using NURBS. The system fine-tunes a large language model to convert text into JSON representations containing NURBS surface parameters (control points, knot vectors, degrees, rational weights) convertible to BRep format. A hybrid representation combines untrimmed NURBS with analytic primitives. We introduce partABC, a curated subset of ABC with individual CAD components.
method_summary: LLM fine-tuned to generate NURBS surface parameters (control points, knots, degrees, weights) as JSON from text descriptions. Hybrid representation combining untrimmed NURBS with analytic primitives for trimmed surfaces. Introduces partABC dataset.

title: Img2CAD: Conditioned 3D CAD Model Generation from Single Image with Structured Visual Geometry
abstract: We identify an innovative intermediate representation called Structured Visual Geometry (SVG), characterized by vectorized wireframes extracted from objects. This representation significantly enhances the generation of editable CAD models from single images. Unlike existing AI methods that rely on mesh-based representations incompatible with CAD tools, Img2CAD generates parametric CAD models with editability and fine control.
method_summary: Uses Structured Visual Geometry (SVG) as intermediate representation - vectorized wireframes extracted from images. Two-stage pipeline: image to SVG wireframe, then SVG to parametric CAD model. Bridges the gap between image input and editable CAD output.

title: Masked BRep Autoencoder via Hierarchical Graph Transformer
abstract: We introduce a novel self-supervised learning framework that automatically learns representations from input computer-aided design (CAD) models for downstream tasks, including part classification, model retrieval, and segmentation. The framework uses masked autoencoding on B-rep structures with a hierarchical graph transformer architecture.
method_summary: Self-supervised masked autoencoding framework for B-rep CAD models using hierarchical graph transformer. Learns general-purpose B-rep representations for downstream tasks (classification, retrieval, segmentation) without task-specific supervision.

title: CADFusion: Text-to-CAD Generation Through Infusing Visual Feedback in Large Language Models
abstract: We introduce CADFusion, a framework that uses Large Language Models as the backbone and alternates between two training stages: the sequential learning (SL) stage and the visual feedback (VF) stage. The SL stage trains the LLM to generate parametric CAD sequences from text. The VF stage renders generated models and uses visual comparison to refine generation quality.
method_summary: LLM-based text-to-CAD with alternating sequential learning and visual feedback training stages. Sequential stage learns text-to-CAD-sequence mapping; visual feedback stage renders outputs and uses visual comparison to improve geometric accuracy.

key_results.approach: visual feedback loop for LLM-based CAD

title: Img2CAD: Reverse Engineering 3D CAD Models from Images through VLM-Assisted Conditional Factorization
abstract: We present Img2CAD, a framework for reverse engineering 3D CAD models from single-view images. Our method leverages GPT-4V to predict the discrete CAD program structure and then uses a semantic-conditioned transformer to predict continuous geometric attributes. This factorization into discrete structure (via VLM) and continuous parameters (via specialized network) enables effective image-to-CAD conversion.
method_summary: Factorizes image-to-CAD into discrete structure prediction (via GPT-4V vision-language model) and continuous attribute prediction (via semantic-conditioned transformer). VLM handles high-level program structure while specialized network handles precise geometry.

title: SolidGen: An Autoregressive Model for Direct B-rep Synthesis
abstract: We present SolidGen, an autoregressive neural network that models the B-rep directly by predicting the vertices, edges, and faces using Transformer-based and pointer neural networks. The approach enables learning and synthesizing boundary representations without requiring supervision through CAD modeling sequences. A key innovation is the Indexed Boundary Representation, which references B-rep vertices, edges and faces in a well-defined hierarchy for machine learning. The model supports conditioning on class labels, images, and voxels.
method_summary: Autoregressive Transformer with pointer networks that directly generates B-rep elements (vertices, edges, faces) in sequence. Introduces Indexed Boundary Representation for ML-friendly B-rep encoding. First direct B-rep synthesis without CAD command sequences.

title: CAD-MLLM: Unifying Multimodality-Conditioned CAD Generation With MLLM
abstract: We present a unified CAD generation system capable of producing parametric CAD models from diverse user inputs including text descriptions, images, point clouds, or combinations thereof. The framework leverages command sequences and large language models to align multimodal data with CAD representations. We introduce Omni-CAD, the first multimodal CAD dataset containing textual description, multi-view images, points, and command sequence for each CAD model with approximately 450K instances.
method_summary: Multimodal LLM-based CAD generation system that aligns text, image, and point cloud inputs with CAD command sequences. Frozen encoders with projection layers map each modality to LLM token space. Introduces Omni-CAD dataset (450K multimodal CAD instances).

title: ExtrudeNet: Unsupervised Inverse Sketch-and-Extrude for Shape Parsing
abstract: Sketch-and-extrude is a common and intuitive modeling process in computer aided design. This paper studies the problem of learning the shape given in the form of point clouds by inverse sketch-and-extrude. We introduce ExtrudeNet, an unsupervised network that discovers sketch and extrude operations from point clouds. Key contributions include a representation supporting freeform sketches and conventional primitives, plus a numerical method for computing signed distance fields.
method_summary: Unsupervised network that decomposes point clouds into sketch-and-extrude operations. Supports freeform sketches and conventional primitives (cylinders, boxes). Produces compact, editable CAD-compatible representations without supervision.

title: AGDC: Autoregressive Generation of Variable-Length Sequences with Joint Discrete and Continuous Spaces
abstract: Transformer-based autoregressive models excel in data generation but are inherently constrained by their reliance on discretized tokens, which limits their ability to represent continuous values with high precision. We propose AGDC, a framework combining categorical prediction for discrete values with diffusion-based modeling for continuous values. Key innovations include end-of-sequence logit adjustment and length regularization. We introduce ContLayNet, a large-scale benchmark with 334K semiconductor layout samples.
method_summary: Unified framework combining categorical prediction for discrete tokens with diffusion-based generation for continuous values in autoregressive sequences. Handles variable-length sequences with mixed discrete-continuous data types. Applicable to CAD-like parametric sequences.
